description:
You are an MLOps engineer who must perform a very quick security-hygiene check over the
    current set of experiment artifacts.  
    A colleague requested a scan for hard-coded application secrets
    (specifically the string “SECRET_KEY=…”) inside every text file that lives
    under /home/user/experiments.

    Your objectives:

    1. Recursively scan /home/user/experiments for any line that contains exactly the
       substring “SECRET_KEY=” (case-sensitive).  
       • A match is valid no matter what characters come after the equal sign.  
       • Binary files should be ignored; conf, txt, env, yaml, json or any other
         plain-text file types must be considered.

    2. Create the directory /home/user/security_scan (it does **not** exist yet).

    3. Inside that directory produce a comma-separated report file named
       /home/user/security_scan/secret_key_scan.csv whose content follows
       **precisely** this format (the test harness will fail on extra whitespace,
       different separators, missing final newline, etc.):

            file_path,line_number,matched_text
            <absolute path of file>,<decimal line number>,<full matched line text>
            <absolute path of file>,<decimal line number>,<full matched line text>
            …

       • The very first line must be the header shown above.  
       • Each subsequent line represents one match.  
       • The “matched_text” column must contain the entire original line exactly as
         it appears in the file (including “SECRET_KEY=” and anything that follows)
         but **without** leading or trailing spaces.  
       • Lines must be ordered by ascending file path; where two matches are in the
         same file, order them by ascending line number.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

    4. Produce a human-readable summary file
       /home/user/security_scan/scan_summary.log with exactly three lines in the
       order shown below (again, no extra whitespace and a trailing newline is
       required):

            Total files scanned: <integer>
            Total matches found: <integer>
            Scan completed at: <RFC-3339 timestamp>

       • The timestamp must use the output of the command `date --rfc-3339=seconds`
         (e.g. “2023-08-19 14:33:57+00:00”).  
       • Ensure the integer counts are correct with respect to what actually exists
         under /home/user/experiments.

    The whole task can be accomplished with 2-3 shell commands (think mkdir,
    grep/awk, wc, date, etc.).  Use whatever you prefer as long as the output
    files **exactly** match the formats above.
truth:
Initial filesystem setup (already present before the agent starts):

    1. Directory /home/user/experiments exists and contains:
       a) /home/user/experiments/run_01/config.env
          Contents (with trailing newline):
              DB_PASSWORD=mydbpw
              SECRET_KEY=abc123xyz

       b) /home/user/experiments/run_01/notes.txt
          Contents:
              Training accuracy reached 0.93
              Need to regenerate data

       c) /home/user/experiments/run_02/config.env
          Contents:
              API_TOKEN=notasecret
              ENDPOINT=https://api.internal

       d) /home/user/experiments/run_02/metrics.json
          Contents:
              {
                "loss": 0.123,
                "accuracy": 0.932
              }

    2. No directory named /home/user/security_scan is present yet.

    Expected final state after correct execution:

    1. Directory /home/user/security_scan is newly created with default
       user-writable permissions.

    2. /home/user/security_scan/secret_key_scan.csv exists and contains
       exactly the following five lines (including the final newline):

        file_path,line_number,matched_text
        /home/user/experiments/run_01/config.env,2,SECRET_KEY=abc123xyz

       (Note: there is a single trailing newline after line 2.)

    3. /home/user/security_scan/scan_summary.log exists and contains three lines,
       for example:

        Total files scanned: 4
        Total matches found: 1
        Scan completed at: 2023-08-19 14:33:57+00:00

       • The first integer must be “4” because exactly four regular files reside
         under /home/user/experiments at start.  
       • The second integer must be “1” because only one “SECRET_KEY=” match
         exists.  
       • The timestamp must be a valid RFC-3339 time equal to the moment the agent
         ran the command (any correct second is accepted by the grader).

    4. No other files or directories should be created or modified.
